_rationale: Stylelint config. The Frontend/ subdirectory is the default Create-React-App boilerplate. CRA's official style guide uses camelCase class names (`.App`, `.App-header`, `.App-logo`) which the upstream toolchain also enforces. The scorecard's `Stylelint_selector-class-pattern` alert is a *style* disagreement, not a security issue; rewriting the boilerplate to kebab-case would diverge from the CRA convention that the CTF challenge explicitly references. Each rule below is documented.

selector-class-pattern._doc: Disabled: CRA convention is PascalCase (`.App`, `.App-header`). Forcing kebab-case would require a wholesale rewrite of the CRA boilerplate; the CTF challenge explicitly references 'CRA's default styling' as the starting point. This is a documented style choice, not a bug.

font-family-name-quotes._doc: Disabled: CRA's default index.css uses bare font names (`Oxygen`, `Ubuntu`) which the original CRA template ships unquoted. Adding quotes here would diverge from the upstream CRA boilerplate that the CTF challenge references.

keyframes-name-pattern._doc: Disabled: the keyframe `App-logo-spin` is defined and consumed in the same file (App.css + App.js) and follows the same PascalCase convention as the class selectors. The pattern is consistent within the file; changing it would break the local reference.
